metadata.source_report: Tyr Group Biological Treatment Process Design Report Rev B, Jan 2012
metadata.critical_constraint: TN = 5 mg/L median is HARD LIMIT (non-negotiable)
metadata.critical_note_for_claude: Canungra is 5-STAGE BARDENPHO not MLE. Two distinct denit zones. Use full mass balance across both anoxic zones, not MLE effluent formulas.
metadata.critical_constraint_v2: Membrane tank volume is SET by membrane area × packing density requirements at the target EP. Cannot arbitrarily shrink membrane tank to free volume for biology without proving membrane area still meets hydraulic demand.
metadata.preferred_concept_notes: Two preferred concepts sit alongside each other pending Phase 2 verification. S2-C3 has conservative post-anoxic sizing (142 kL effective). S2-E has simpler process flow with flow balancing (requires RFC-12). Choice between them depends on RFC-04 (S2-C3 hydraulics) and RFC-12 (S2-E flow balancing).

S0_rev_b_baseline.description: Rev B as designed and constructed
S0_rev_b_baseline.as_built_note: Two 59 kL MBR tanks per civil drawing 8720Y-050. Combined 118 kL (Rev B stated 130 kL includes plenum/piping).

S1A_controls_only.description: S1A — Controls and operational upgrades only. Flow-paced all three recycles, instrumentation audit, SRT/DO optimisation. No IFAS carriers. No civil work.

recycles._note: Rev B ratios retained; only control mode changes to flow-paced

S1B_controls_plus_ifas.description: S1B — S1A plus IFAS carriers in aerobic zone (conditional: requires nitrification audit showing IFAS headroom adds value).

recycles._note: Rev B ratios retained; only control mode changes to flow-paced

S2_A_combined.description: S2-A: Remove internal MBR wall to create combined 156 kL post-anoxic (38 existing + 118 former MBR bay). HF MBR in new ~20 kL tank. Single MeOH dosing point. Optimised recycles (A=10x, S=5x, R=1x from post-anoxic).

membrane.design_note: New HF MBR tank ~20 kL. Supports up to ~5000 EP hydraulically.

recycles._design_basis: A=10x reduced from Rev B 14x (saves ~28% pumping, costs ~2.5% capacity). S=5x reduced from 6x (better HF MLSS match, 17% pump saving). R-recycle redirected to post-anoxic outlet for near-zero NO3 in anaerobic zone (improves bio-P, reduces alum).

S2_A_combined.as_built_advantage: Tank 2 already exists with bottom slope, inlet/outlet, freeboard. Civil work = baffle wall, mixer install, pipe rework. NOT a new tank construction.

S2_B_parallel.description: S2-B: Parallel post-anoxic 38+59+59 kL with wall retained. Flow distribution chamber splits flow 24:38:38. Three MeOH dosing points proportional to flow. Biologically equivalent to S2-A, adds +AUD 263k for wall retention + flow split.

membrane.design_note: New HF MBR tank ~20 kL. Supports up to ~5000 EP hydraulically.

recycles._design_basis: A=10x reduced from Rev B 14x (saves ~28% pumping, costs ~2.5% capacity). S=5x reduced from 6x (better HF MLSS match, 17% pump saving). R-recycle redirected to post-anoxic outlet for near-zero NO3 in anaerobic zone (improves bio-P, reduces alum).

S2_B_parallel.as_built_advantage: Tank 2 already exists with bottom slope, inlet/outlet, freeboard. Civil work = baffle wall, mixer install, pipe rework. NOT a new tank construction.
S2_B_parallel.note: Same total post-anoxic volume (156 kL) as S2-A, so same biology. Parallel hydraulic config.

S2_C3_series_parallel.description: S2-C3: RECOMMENDED. Series 38 kL endogenous pre-stage then 50:50 split to parallel 59+59 kL cells with MeOH dosing. No MeOH at 38 kL. Two dosing points (50:50 symmetric). No wall removal. Effective post-anox ~142 kL.

membrane.design_note: New HF MBR tank ~20 kL. Supports up to ~5000 EP hydraulically.

recycles._design_basis: A=10x reduced from Rev B 14x (saves ~28% pumping, costs ~2.5% capacity). S=5x reduced from 6x (better HF MLSS match, 17% pump saving). R-recycle redirected to post-anoxic outlet for near-zero NO3 in anaerobic zone (improves bio-P, reduces alum).

S2_C3_series_parallel.as_built_advantage: Tank 2 already exists with bottom slope, inlet/outlet, freeboard. Civil work = baffle wall, mixer install, pipe rework. NOT a new tank construction.
S2_C3_series_parallel.note: RECOMMENDED S2 variant. Effective 142 kL captures endogenous value of 38 kL chamber without MeOH there.

S2_D_parallel_only.description: S2-D: Parallel-only 59+59 kL cells with 38 kL chamber decommissioned (repurposed). Two MeOH dosing points (50:50 symmetric). Lowest cost S2 variant. 118 kL total post-anoxic.

membrane.design_note: New HF MBR tank ~20 kL. Supports up to ~5000 EP hydraulically.

recycles._design_basis: A=10x reduced from Rev B 14x (saves ~28% pumping, costs ~2.5% capacity). S=5x reduced from 6x (better HF MLSS match, 17% pump saving). R-recycle redirected to post-anoxic outlet for near-zero NO3 in anaerobic zone (improves bio-P, reduces alum).

S2_D_parallel_only.as_built_advantage: Tank 2 already exists with bottom slope, inlet/outlet, freeboard. Civil work = baffle wall, mixer install, pipe rework. NOT a new tank construction.
S2_D_parallel_only.note: Lowest-cost S2 variant. 38 kL chamber decommissioned for other use.

S2_E_aerobic_shifted.description: S2-E: Aerobic-shifted with flow balancing. Existing 38 kL post-anoxic CONVERTED to aerobic (aerobic = 117 + 38 = 155 kL, +32%). Former MBR bay (2x59 kL = 118 kL) becomes single post-anoxic zone. New MBR structure incorporates flow balancing (~10 kL balance + ~20 kL membrane). Single MeOH dosing point. No wall removal required.

membrane.design_note: New HF MBR tank ~20 kL. Supports up to ~5000 EP hydraulically.

recycles._design_basis: A=10x reduced from Rev B 14x (saves ~28% pumping, costs ~2.5% capacity). S=5x reduced from 6x (better HF MLSS match, 17% pump saving). R-recycle redirected to post-anoxic outlet for near-zero NO3 in anaerobic zone (improves bio-P, reduces alum).

S2_E_aerobic_shifted.as_built_advantage: Tank 2 already exists with bottom slope, inlet/outlet, freeboard. Civil work = baffle wall, mixer install, pipe rework. NOT a new tank construction.

flow_balancing.note: Flow balancing attenuates MML peak loading. Capacity uplift from ~4,500 EP (S2-D equivalent, no balance) to ~5,700 EP at 2-4 hour balance retention. Requires RFC-12 dynamic simulation to confirm.

S2_E_aerobic_shifted.note: Fifth S2 variant added Rev 20. Flow balancing is load-bearing for capacity recovery: without balance tank, capacity = S2-D (4,500 EP). RFC-12 dynamic simulation required before capital commitment.

S2_ifas_plus_postanox.description: S2-A: Remove internal MBR wall to create combined 156 kL post-anoxic (38 existing + 118 former MBR bay). HF MBR in new ~20 kL tank. Single MeOH dosing point. Optimised recycles (A=10x, S=5x, R=1x from post-anoxic).

membrane.design_note: New HF MBR tank ~20 kL. Supports up to ~5000 EP hydraulically.

recycles._design_basis: A=10x reduced from Rev B 14x (saves ~28% pumping, costs ~2.5% capacity). S=5x reduced from 6x (better HF MLSS match, 17% pump saving). R-recycle redirected to post-anoxic outlet for near-zero NO3 in anaerobic zone (improves bio-P, reduces alum).

S2_ifas_plus_postanox.as_built_advantage: Tank 2 already exists with bottom slope, inlet/outlet, freeboard. Civil work = baffle wall, mixer install, pipe rework. NOT a new tank construction.

kinetic_parameters._provenance: Confirmed SE QLD values per ph2o Consulting (P. Hillis, April 2026). Tyr Group Rev B BioWin used defaults with one explicit Kn change. Values below reflect SE QLD calibration.
kinetic_parameters._calibration_status: VALIDATED for Rev B baseline at 1500 EP: TN ~ 1.85-3.5 mg/L vs Rev B BioWin band 2.5-4.0 (slightly optimistic at low end but inside band)
kinetic_parameters._Kn_note: Rev B explicit deviation from BioWin default of 0.7 mgN/L
_se_qld_calibration: K2 primary anoxic (RBCOD): 0.10-0.12 gN/gVSS/d at 20°C — confirmed for SE QLD biomass
K3 endogenous post-anox: 0.05 gN/gVSS/d at 20°C — standard endogenous rate
K3 MeOH-acclimated: 0.10-0.15 gN/gVSS/d at 20°C — typical for acclimated methylotrophs
kinetic_parameters._remaining_uncertainty: With SE QLD K2/K3 applied, dominant remaining uncertainty is K2_MeOH_acclimated (0.10-0.15 range). SBCOD_hydrolysis_boost (1.0-1.4) is second-order. Bench test on Canungra biomass would tighten K2_MeOH.

DIL_framework_inputs.notes: Maps this analysis to WaterPoint 9-part Decision Intelligence Layer
DIL_framework_inputs.decision_question: Should Canungra STP be intensified to 3000+ EP using IFAS + enlarged post-anoxic + HF MBR, or should an expansion/greenfield be planned?
key_uncertainties: IFAS biofilm nitrification flux at 17C (range 0.4-0.9 gN/m2/d)
DIL_framework_inputs.binding_constraints_per_scenario: To be populated from simulation outputs

description.A: 607 kg TN/yr hard cap regardless of EP (conservative interpretation)
description.B: 607 kg/yr scales with design EP: limit = 607 * (EP / 1500) kg/yr
